title: MDS Connector Manifest
description: Declarative manifest for a connector in the connectors-library submodule. This is the single source of truth consumed by the frontend catalog, the deterministic DDL generator (Manifest.to_ddl), the connector dispatcher, and CI validation. See docs/architecture.md §3.2.

id.description: Globally unique manifest id, snake_case. Used as the primary key in /api/catalog and as the BigQuery table suffix. Example: 'meta_facebook_insights'.

name.description: Human-readable name shown in the frontend catalog. Example: 'Facebook Ads Insights'.

platform.description: Top-level grouping key for the frontend catalog. Maps to the parent folder inside the submodule. Examples: 'meta', 'google_ads', 'dv360'.

connector.description: Specific connector identifier within a platform. Maps to the second-level folder. Examples: 'facebook', 'ig', 'reports'.

version.description: Semantic version of the manifest contract. Bump major when removing fields or changing the params shape; bump minor when adding optional fields or params; bump patch for documentation/metadata changes.

description.description: Optional long-form description shown in the frontend tooltip.

owner.description: Optional team or person responsible for keeping this connector working.

status.description: Lifecycle stage. The frontend can hide non-stable connectors behind a feature flag if desired.

endpoint.description: How to invoke this connector. The dispatcher uses 'module_path' + 'function_name' in LocalBackend (dev) and 'cloud_function_url' in HTTPBackend (prod).

module_path.description: Importable Python module path relative to the submodule root, used by LocalBackend via importlib. Example: 'meta.facebook.facebook_ads'.

function_name.description: Name of the function exported by 'module_path' that implements the contract fetch(params, context) -> {status, code, records, meta, errors}.

cloud_function_url.description: URL of the deployed Cloud Function for HTTPBackend. Per-tenant URL is allowed — see docs/architecture.md §5 for impersonation flow.

cloud_function_name.description: Logical name of the CF used by deploy scripts.

cloud_function_region.description: GCP region of the deployed Cloud Function.

auth.description: Authentication contract: what TenantContext must inject into 'context' before invoking the connector.

context_required.description: List of keys that must be present in the 'context' arg of fetch(). The dispatcher will refuse to invoke if any are missing. Example: ['ad_account_id', 'access_token'].

secrets.description: Mapping from context keys to Secret Manager resources. The dispatcher resolves these via TenantContext.

secret_id.description: Secret Manager resource id (without project prefix). Example: 'meta-access-token'.

scopes.description: OAuth scopes required by this connector. Documentation only; not enforced by the dispatcher.

params.description: Input parameters accepted by the connector. The validator node uses this to reject malformed requests before invocation.

one_of.description: Mutually-exclusive parameter groups. Exactly one group must be fully present. Useful when a connector accepts e.g. ['date_start','date_stop'] OR ['since','until'].

available_fields.description: Catalog of fields the connector can return. The frontend uses this to render the field selector. Manifest.to_ddl uses (name, type, mode, description) to emit BigQuery DDL.

name.description: Field name as it appears in the connector's response and as the BigQuery column name.

type.description: BigQuery type. Manifest.to_ddl emits this verbatim.

description.description: Optional column description, included in the BigQuery DDL OPTIONS clause.

items_type.description: When type=ARRAY, the type of the array elements (e.g. 'STRING').

fields.description: When type=STRUCT, the nested field definitions.

selectable.description: If false, the field is always included (e.g. partition keys) and not exposed in the frontend selector.

table_naming.description: How the destination BigQuery table is named and physically organized. Optional — defaults are derived from id.

bronze_pattern.description: Bronze layer table naming pattern. Tokens: {platform}, {connector}, {id}, {version_major}, {tenant_id}. The {tenant_id} token is substituted at run-time from the active tenant key so multi-tenant deployments can land each customer's data into its own table (e.g. bronze.meta_facebook_ad_insights_cliente1).

partition_field.description: Name of the field used for time partitioning. Must reference a field in 'available_fields' of type DATE/DATETIME/TIMESTAMP.

clustering_fields.description: Up to 4 fields used for clustering. BigQuery enforces this maximum.

limits.description: Operational limits the dispatcher should respect.

metadata.description: Free-form key/value pairs for connector-specific context (e.g. API version, doc URLs).

type.description: Logical type for validation. 'field_list' is special: the validator checks that every entry exists in available_fields (used for the canonical 'fields' param).

items_type.description: When type=array, the type of array elements (e.g. 'string').

default.description: Default value used when the param is omitted. Must match 'type'.

pattern.description: Regex constraint, applied to the stringified value when type=string.
